engineIntelligence.h2: Jeep Grand Wagoneer Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Jeep Grand Wagoneer in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data for import vehicles.

cards.altText: Jeep Grand Wagoneer EZH engine - petrol 5.7L replacement UK
productionHistory: Jeep Grand Wagoneer Series I 5.7L HEMI eTorque - 2022-2024
Jeep Grand Wagoneer Series II 5.7L HEMI eTorque - 2022-2024
Jeep Grand Wagoneer L extended wheelbase 5.7L HEMI - 2022-2024
knownFailures: eTorque 48V electrical system faults - belted alternator starter failures reported from 40k miles on US imports.
Exhaust manifold bolt wear - causes characteristic HEMI tick noise, often requires upgraded hardware on UK climate.
ECU immobiliser pairing issues - replacement engines require specialist calibration for UK registration compliance.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £4,500-£8,500 (supply only) Estimated from comparable EZH import units - actual quotes may vary due to US-spec calibration requirements.

cards.altText: Jeep Grand Wagoneer GME engine - petrol 3.0L replacement UK
productionHistory: Jeep Grand Wagoneer Series III Hurricane Standard Output - 2022-2024
Jeep Grand Wagoneer Limited Altitude 3.0L Hurricane - 2022-2024
Jeep Grand Wagoneer L Hurricane twin-turbo - 2022-2024
knownFailures: Twin-turbo wastegate actuator wear - boost pressure loss reported on high-mileage UK import examples.
Intercooler condensation in UK climate - thermal management adaptation recommended post-replacement.
Wiring harness compatibility checks - US-spec looms may require verification for UK electrical integration.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £5,500-£10,500 (supply only) Estimated from comparable GME import units - twin-turbo specialist calibration may affect final pricing.

cards.altText: Jeep Grand Wagoneer GME-HO engine - petrol 3.0L replacement UK
productionHistory: Jeep Grand Wagoneer Summit Reserve Hurricane High Output - 2023-2024
Jeep Grand Wagoneer Obsidian Edition 540hp twin-turbo - 2024
knownFailures: High-output turbo bearing wear - increased stress on UK stop-start driving patterns requires specialist monitoring.
HO-specific ECU calibration requirements - software updates essential post-replacement for UK emissions compliance.
Regenerative braking system integration - eTorque hybrid calibration must be verified by IMI-certified technicians.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £7,500-£14,000 (supply only) Premium high-output units - scarcity and specialist calibration requirements affect pricing for Summit Reserve variants.

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol

compatibleVariants: Series I, Series II, Series I L, Series II L, Limited Altitude, Summit, Summit Reserve
commonFailures: eTorque 48V belted alternator starter failures reported from 40k miles on US imports
Exhaust manifold bolt wear causing characteristic HEMI tick noise on UK climate exposure
ECU immobiliser pairing requires specialist calibration for UK registration compliance

compatibleVariants: Series II, Series II L, Summit Reserve 392, Obsidian Edition
commonFailures: High-performance valve train wear accelerated by UK stop-start driving patterns
Oil consumption monitoring recommended beyond 50k miles on high-output applications
Specialist calibration required for UK emissions testing post-replacement

commonProblems.h2: Jeep Grand Wagoneer Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Jeep Grand Wagoneer's HEMI eTorque and Hurricane twin-turbo engines dominate UK import replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from vetted UK specialists.

problems.group: Jeep Grand Wagoneer eTorque 48V Electrical Failure
problems.h4: Jeep Grand Wagoneer eTorque 48V Electrical Failure - Why EZH HEMI Engines Develop No-Start Faults
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Grand Wagoneer Series I, Series II, L variants (2022-2024, EZH 5.7L HEMI eTorque)
problems.rootCause: The 48V belted alternator starter (BAS) unit experiences electrical connector degradation and control module faults, particularly on US-spec imports adapted to UK climate conditions.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty 48V belted alternator starter unit with upgraded connector harness
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early before ECU damage; 18-24 month expected lifespan with climate-adapted parts

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace BAS, 48V battery, DC-DC converter and recalibrate eTorque control software
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive solution for multiple electrical faults; suitable for vehicles worth £45k+

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EZH Engine with eTorque
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EZH engine with upgraded eTorque components, UK calibration, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage imports or catastrophic electrical failure; includes all upgraded components

problems.recommendation: On a Jeep Grand Wagoneer worth £45,000-£75,000, if eTorque faults appear before 40k miles, the minor BAS replacement offers better value. Beyond 50k miles or with multiple electrical faults, a rebuilt EZH engine with upgraded eTorque components provides longer-term reliability and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EZH eTorque engine prices for your Jeep Grand Wagoneer

problems.group: Jeep Grand Wagoneer Hurricane Twin-Turbo Boost Loss
problems.h4: Jeep Grand Wagoneer Hurricane Twin-Turbo Boost Loss - Why GME Engines Lose Power in UK Climate
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Grand Wagoneer Series III, Limited Altitude, Summit variants (2022-2024, GME 3.0L Hurricane)
problems.rootCause: Twin-turbo wastegate actuators experience wear from UK stop-start driving patterns; intercooler condensation in cooler UK climate accelerates turbo bearing wear on US-spec imports.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Actuator Calibration & Intercooler Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Recalibrate wastegate actuators, flush intercooler system, update ECU boost maps
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for early-stage boost loss; requires specialist Hurricane calibration equipment

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Twin-Turbo Rebuild with UK Climate Adaptation
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuild both turbochargers with UK-spec bearings, upgrade intercooler drainage, recalibrate ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause of climate-related wear; 30k+ mile expected extension with proper maintenance

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt GME Hurricane Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt GME engine with climate-adapted twin-turbo system, UK calibration, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage imports or catastrophic turbo failure; includes all upgraded components

problems.recommendation: For Jeep Grand Wagoneer Hurricane engines showing boost loss before 45k miles, the intermediate twin-turbo rebuild offers the best balance of cost and longevity. Beyond 60k miles or with bearing noise, a rebuilt GME engine with UK climate-adapted components provides superior long-term value.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt GME Hurricane engine prices for your Jeep Grand Wagoneer

problems.group: Jeep Grand Wagoneer HEMI Exhaust Manifold Tick
problems.h4: Jeep Grand Wagoneer HEMI Exhaust Manifold Tick - Why EZH/ESF Engines Develop Rattle Noise
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Grand Wagoneer Series I, Series II, L variants (2022-2024, EZH 5.7L / ESF 6.4L HEMI)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust manifold studs and bolts experience thermal cycling fatigue; UK climate temperature variations accelerate bolt stretch and gasket degradation on US-spec imports.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Exhaust Manifold Bolt Upgrade Kit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace factory bolts with upgraded hardware, install revised gaskets, torque to UK-spec procedure
repairOptions.longevity: Effective preventative fix if caught early; 25k+ mile expected extension with proper installation

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Manifold Replacement with UK Spec Hardware
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace both exhaust manifolds with UK-spec upgraded units, revised gaskets, full system calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive solution for persistent tick; suitable for vehicles with 40k+ miles

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt HEMI Engine with Upgraded Exhaust System
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EZH/ESF engine with upgraded exhaust manifold hardware, UK calibration, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage imports or multiple concurrent failures; includes all upgraded components

problems.recommendation: For HEMI tick appearing before 35k miles, the minor bolt upgrade kit offers excellent value and prevents escalation. Beyond 45k miles or with concurrent oil consumption issues, a rebuilt HEMI engine with upgraded exhaust hardware provides superior long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt HEMI engine prices for your Jeep Grand Wagoneer

problems.group: Jeep Grand Wagoneer Hurricane HO High-Output Calibration Issues
problems.h4: Jeep Grand Wagoneer Hurricane HO High-Output Calibration Issues - Why GME-HO Engines Require Specialist Attention
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Grand Wagoneer Summit Reserve, Obsidian Edition (2023-2024, GME-HO 3.0L Hurricane High Output)
problems.rootCause: High-output twin-turbo calibration requires precise ECU software matching; US-spec imports may experience adaptation drift in UK driving conditions, triggering performance limiting or fault codes.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - HO ECU Recalibration & Adaptation Reset
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Perform HO-specific ECU software update, reset adaptation values, verify twin-turbo calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for early-stage calibration drift; requires specialist Hurricane HO diagnostic equipment

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - HO Turbo System Recalibration with UK Climate Adaptation
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Recalibrate both high-output turbos, update HO-specific ECU maps, adapt for UK climate driving patterns
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause of performance limiting; 35k+ mile expected extension with proper maintenance

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt GME-HO Hurricane HO Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt GME-HO engine with UK climate-adapted HO calibration, specialist certification, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for Summit Reserve/Obsidian imports with high-mileage or catastrophic failure; includes all upgraded components

problems.recommendation: For Hurricane HO engines showing calibration faults before 30k miles, the minor ECU recalibration offers excellent value. Beyond 40k miles or with performance limiting, a rebuilt GME-HO engine with UK climate-adapted HO calibration provides superior long-term reliability for these premium import vehicles.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt GME-HO Hurricane HO engine prices for your Jeep Grand Wagoneer

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Jeep Grand Wagoneer
finalCta.paragraph: The Jeep Grand Wagoneer's sophisticated HEMI eTorque and Hurricane twin-turbo engines demand specialist attention when issues arise. EngineMarket connects you with UK import specialists who understand US-spec calibration requirements, offer rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing known failure points, and provide nationwide supply & fit with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices from vetted specialists today and protect your investment without paying main dealer premiums.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Jeep Grand Wagoneer Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Jeep Grand Wagoneer and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.question: How much does a Jeep Grand Wagoneer engine replacement cost in the UK for import vehicles?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt engine prices range from £4,500 for 5.7L HEMI eTorque to £14,000 for Hurricane High Output units, depending on engine code, variant and calibration requirements for UK registration.

GME-HO Hurricane HO: £7,500-£14,000 rebuilt supply only

All prices include UK calibration and 12-month warranty

items.question: What engine code does my imported Jeep Grand Wagoneer have?
items.answer: Use our free VIN checker above to instantly identify your engine code - EZH for 5.7L HEMI eTorque, ESF for 6.4L HEMI 392, or GME/GME-HO for 3.0L Hurricane variants.
keyPoints: EZH = 5.7L HEMI with eTorque mild hybrid (Series I/II)
ESF = 6.4L HEMI 392 performance V8 (Series II/Obsidian)
GME = 3.0L Hurricane Standard Output twin-turbo I6 (Series III)
GME-HO = 3.0L Hurricane High Output twin-turbo (Summit Reserve)
VIN verification essential for UK import compatibility confirmation
